_provenance.head_type: PURE-MATH fleet-external head (kin: the P028/P032/P048/P060/P072/P076 exact-counting family) - no lane pin, no vendor tree, every registered numeral invented-but-pinned in the idea file itself; zero repo/network reads at verdict time

_provenance.rational_convention: all decision arithmetic is seedless exact integer/Fraction arithmetic; no floats in any decision path; irrational-valued anchors are registered as DECIMAL STRINGS at a stated precision and matched by round-half-up on the exact Fraction (see _disclosures.decimal_display)

world.definition: m = 2n boxes and players; one uniform random permutation pi of {0..m-1} (box b holds number pi(b)); each player opens up to b boxes ADAPTIVELY; group success = every player finds their own number

strategies.pointer: start at own label, follow contents (player i succeeds iff the cycle of pi containing i has length <= b; the group succeeds iff no cycle exceeds b)
strategies.independent: each player an independent uniform n-subset - joint success exactly 2^-m given ANY fixed arrangement (pencil, F4d)
strategies.same_set: all open one fixed n-set - joint success exactly 0 (pigeonhole, F4c)
strategies.one_sided_remap: shared uniform sigma: start at sigma(i), read content c as sigma(c) - effective permutation pi o sigma, uniform for ANY fixed pi
strategies.conjugation: shared relabeling sigma^-1 o pi o sigma - the folk repair, priced as the cycle-type-preserving no-op it is

world.adversary_witness_m100: any single 100-cycle (the sim uses the rotation i -> i+1 mod 100)

laws.P_n: P_n = 1 - T_n = joint pointer success at m = 2n, b = n
laws.harmonic_law: for b >= n: joint pointer success = 1 - sum_{k=b+1}^{m} 1/k exactly (at most one cycle can exceed b; #{perms with a cycle of length exactly k > b} = m!/k)
laws.uniform_cycle_length_law: the cycle length of a FIXED element of a uniform permutation is uniform on {1..m}: exactly (m-1)! permutations per length
laws.marginal_law: each player's own success at budget b is exactly b/m
laws.decrement_identity: T_{n+1} - T_n = 1/((2n+1)(2n+2)), verified exactly for n = 1..199
laws.alternating_identity: T_n = sum_{j=1}^{2n} (-1)^{j+1}/j (the even partial sums of the alternating harmonic series - the pencil route to T_n < ln 2 forever), verified exactly for n = 1..199

anchors_F3.repair_one_sided_note: 276/720 against EVERY pinned arrangement (identity, 6-cycle, 3+3) - equality with the uniform census BY THE COMPOSITION THEOREM
anchors_F3.adversarial_note: a single 2n-cycle zeroes the deterministic pointer - all 2n players fail at once
anchors_F3.floor: P_n > 1 - ln 2 forever, certified by a rational ln-2 bracket

contacts.C1: three-method counting triangle: brute joint census == m! * P_{m/2} at m in {4, 6, 8} (10 / 276 / 14736) AND partition census == brute at (8, 4) AND partition census at m = 10 == 1285920 == 10! * 893/2520 - three independent counting methods, one number
contacts.C2: per-length counts of a fixed element's cycle == (m-1)! each, summing to m! (m in {4, 6, 8})
contacts.C3: one-sided-remap census == 276 for ALL THREE pinned arrangements; conjugation census == {720, 0, 720}, frozen to cycle type
contacts.C4: true census == naive harmonic + the exact inclusion-exclusion term at (10, 4) (+10!/50) and (8, 3) (+8!/32; the (8, 3) partition census additionally confirmed by brute force)

arm_r.grammar: m = 100 episodes; one Fisher-Yates permutation per episode: p = list(range(100)); for i in range(99, 0, -1): j = rng.randrange(i + 1); swap p[i], p[j] - exactly m - 1 = 99 randrange draws per episode, i from 99 down to 1; ONE random.Random(seed) per seed for all N episodes; REPORTING-ONLY, no statistical gate
arm_r.triple_definition: (wins, failing_episodes, total_failing_player_mass) where a win is max cycle <= 50 and the failing-player mass of a failing episode is the number of players on cycles longer than 50

20261717.role: aux - reserved, NEVER read by any leg

arm_r.allocation_note: seeds 20261714-717 allocated from 20261714 per the P079 card's baton: 20261710-713 are P079/V092's registered set (aux 20261713 never read), so 20261714 is the next free index
arm_r.sentinel: draw counter == exactly 99 * N per seed (1,980,000 / 792,000)
arm_r.cpython_pin: 3.11 (the minor this verdict session runs and asserts; Arms A/B are platform-independent exact arithmetic - only Arm R and the presentation shuffle depend on the pinned minor's random module)

decision_rule.order: REJECT -> INVALID -> APPROVE -> NULL (REJECT evaluated FIRST)
decision_rule.REJECT: R1 AND R2 AND R3 AND R4
decision_rule.R1_lever: joint pointer success == 1 - sum_{k=b+1}^{m} 1/k exactly for b >= n; C1's three counting methods agreeing (10 / 276 / 14736 / 1285920); P50 landing 0.3118278207 with ratio to the independent baseline exactly P50 * 2^100 (approx 3.953e29) and the same-set pole exactly 0
decision_rule.R2_marginal_invariance: each player's own success at budget b == b/m exactly at every enumerated m (the (m-1)!-per-length law; 240/360/480 at m = 6)
decision_rule.R3_concentration_floor: E[# failing | joint failure] = 50/T_50 (approx 72.6562 of 100); P_n strictly decreasing with decrement exactly 1/((2n+1)(2n+2)); P_n > 1 - ln 2 certified by the rational bracket (gap at n = 50 approx 0.0049750)
decision_rule.R4_repairs: the adversarial 2n-cycle zeroes the deterministic pointer (all 2n fail at once); the conjugation repair stays 0/720 on the 6-cycle (cycle type frozen: {720, 0, 720} across the three arrangements); the one-sided remap restores exactly 276/720 == the uniform census against EVERY pinned arrangement
decision_rule.INVALID: any F1-F6 gate failure (controls misbehaving - report, no ruling)
decision_rule.APPROVE: the pointer census equals the 2^-m product census at any enumerated m (arithmetically excluded by C1), or some enumerated marginal lands off b/m; mutually exclusive with REJECT
NULL_axes: census-contact mismatch (a C1/C4 counting method disagrees while each arm internally reproduces - the corrected law is the finding)
repair-algebra mismatch (remap != 276 or conjugation off {720, 0, 720} - the corrected algebra is the finding)
floor-bracket failure (the bracket fails its own tail bound - R3's floor clause falls back to the finite decrement law alone)
twin-arm disagreement surviving the INVALID diagnosis

gates.F1: model identities: cycle lengths partition m (sum == m) for every enumerated permutation; factorial counts 24 / 720 / 40320; per-length counts sum to m! with each == (m-1)!; T_n/P_n definitions reproduce P1..P5 = 1/2, 5/12, 23/60, 307/840, 893/2520; Fisher-Yates consumes exactly m - 1 draws per episode
gates.F2: the five structure theorems re-derived from scratch: (a) LEVER - m!/k at m = 6 (180/144/120), the harmonic censuses at m in {4, 6, 8}, the partition census at m = 10, the headline P50/ratio/2^-100; (b) MARGINAL - the uniform-cycle-length law at m in {4, 6, 8}, budget marginals 240/360/480; (c) BREAKDOWN - the below-n corrections at (10, 4) and (8, 3) with brute confirmation at (8, 3); (d) REPAIRS - the adversarial 6-cycle zero, the 720-sigma one-sided and conjugation censuses across all three arrangements; (e) FLOOR - the decrement identity n = 1..199, the ln-2 bracket inside its own tail bound, P50 > 1 - ln 2 with the gap value
gates.F3: census anchors verbatim (the anchors_F3 block above)
gates.F4: pencil worlds: (a) m = 2 lever - pointer P1 = 1/2 vs independent baseline 1/4; (b) the decrement identity in three lines: 1/(2n+1) + 1/(2n+2) - 1/(n+1) == 1/((2n+1)(2n+2)); (c) same-set pigeonhole - joint success 0 by inspection (enumerated); (d) independent baseline given a fixed arrangement == (1/2)^m (enumerated at m = 4: 81 of 1296)
gates.F5: degeneracy controls: b = m gives P = 1; b = m - 1 gives exactly 1 - 1/m; b = 99 at m = 100 == 99/100 exactly; the identity arrangement succeeds under the deterministic pointer and under conjugation for every sigma; the conjugation control on identity and 3+3 (720/720 - relabeling cannot break what cycle type already blessed)
gates.F6: battery: Arm B enumerations equal Arm A closed forms through the FOUR typed contacts C1-C4; twin independently-written decision evaluators agree on the ruling token over an ENUMERATED boolean input set; Arm-R draw-count sentinel (exactly 99N) and exact reproducibility (in-process double run per seed); presentation seed 20261716 read by presentation legs only; aux seed 20261717 never read; stdout + results.json byte-identical across two process runs

_disclosures.note: sim-chosen realizations of shape-registered slots - vacancy-derived disclosures, never match claims
_disclosures.decimal_display: irrational-valued anchors are matched by ROUND-HALF-UP on the exact Fraction at the registered precision: P50 and budget rows at 10 decimal places; the concentration value at 4 decimal places; the floor gap at 7 decimal places; the ratio and the 2^-100 pole as 4-significant-digit mantissa exponent forms (the ratio's integer part is computed exactly and its leading digits rounded)
_disclosures.ln2_bracket_K: K = 60: S_60 = sum_{k=1}^{60} 1/(k*2^k) rational, tail bound 1/(61*2^60); the bracket [S_60, S_60 + tail] certifies ln 2, so T_50 < S_60 certifies P_50 > 1 - ln 2 and the gap bracket is [S_60 - T_50, S_60 + tail - T_50] (width approx 8e-20; any K >= 9 already separates at 7 decimals)
_disclosures.forever_route: the FOREVER half of the floor claim rides the alternating-series pencil argument (T_n is the even partial sum of the alternating harmonic series, increasing to ln 2 from below), gated finitely by the alternating identity + the decrement identity for n = 1..199 plus the n = 50 rational certificate
_disclosures.adversarial_m100_witness: the rotation permutation i -> i+1 mod 100 realizes 'any single 100-cycle'
_disclosures.presentation_leg: seed 20261716 shuffles the display order of the F3 anchor-name roster in ONE reporting-only stdout line (no decision value passes through it)
_disclosures.m4_independent_enumeration: the F4d pencil check enumerates all 6^4 subset choices at m = 4 against one fixed arrangement (success census 81 of 1296 == (1/2)^4)
